memoryArtifacts.headline: Will of Peter Foster dated 1609,  Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England
Will of Peter Foster
memoryArtifacts.description: Will of Peter Foster was probated 13 July 1609.  In it he mentions by name his wife Ellen and children Richard, Thomas, Katherine Tompkins, Elizabeth, Margery Cooke, Isabel,  and Ales Skinner.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Peter Patricke Foster於1550年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾文多福時，他的父親Guy Foster30歲，母親Joan Church27歲。他於1573年11月18日與Helene Greenway在英國巴金哈姆爾文結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1609年7月13日在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾文多福享年59歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾文多福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Peter Patricke Foster于1550年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾文多福时，他的父亲Guy Foster30岁，母亲Joan Church27岁。他于1573年11月18日与Helene Greenway在英国巴金哈姆爾文结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1609年7月13日在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾文多福享年59岁，埋葬在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾文多福。

nameMeanings.name: Foster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Forster   ‘worker in a forest’.
English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English foster ‘foster parent’ (Old English fōstre, a derivative of fōstrian ‘to nourish or rear’). But other explanations are equally or more likely.
English: from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors’; see Forster 2.

variants.name: Fewster
meanings: English (mainly East Yorkshire and Durham): occupational name from Anglo-Norman French and Middle English fu(y)ster ‘maker of saddletrees’. See also Foster .

variants.name: Forster
meanings: English: shortened form of   Forrester  , an occupational name for someone who worked in a forest.
English: possibly a Norman French nickname or occupational name from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors or shears, cutler’. Compare Force and Forse .

German (mainly Förster): agent derivative of Middle High German forst ‘forest’, denoting someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or who worked in one (see Forst ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Foerster .

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Patricke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Name of the apostle and patron saint of Ireland ( c.389–461 ), Gaelic Pádraig. He was a Christian Briton and a Roman citizen, who as a young man was captured and enslaved by raiders from Ireland. He escaped and went to Gaul before returning home to Britain. In about 419 he felt a call to do missionary work in Ireland. He studied for twelve years at Auxerre, and in 432 returned to Ireland. For the rest of his life it is difficult to distinguish fact from fiction. He apparently went to the court of the high kings at Tara and made some converts there; then he travelled around Ireland making further converts until about 445 , when he established his archiepiscopal see at Armagh. By the time of his death almost the whole of Ireland is said to have been converted to Christianity. He is also credited with codifying the laws of Ireland. In his Latin autobiography, as well as in later tradition, his name appears as Patricius ‘patrician’ (i.e. belonging to the Roman senatorial or noble class), but this may actually represent a Latinized form of some lost Celtic (British) name. In Ireland in particular, it has been one of the most enduringly popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Peter Patricke Foster was born in 1550, in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, his father, Guy Foster, was 30 and his mother, Joan Church, was 27. He married Helene Greenway on 18 November 1573, in Wing, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 13 July 1609, in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59, and was buried in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Peter Patricke Foster est né en 1550, à Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Guy Foster, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Joan Church, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Helene Greenway le 18 novembre 1573, à Wing, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 13 juillet 1609, à Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Peter Patricke Foster 1550 in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guy Foster 30 und seine Mutter Joan Church 27. Er hat am 18. November 1573 in Wing, Buckinghamshire, England Helene Greenway geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 13. Juli 1609 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Peter Patricke Foster è nato il nel 1550 nel Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, il suo padre, Guy Foster, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Joan Church, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Helene Greenway il 18 novembre 1573, nel Wing, Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 13 luglio 1609, nel Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Peter Patricke Fosterが1550年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウエンドバーで生まれた時，父Guy Fosterは30歳，母Joan Churchは27歳であった。1573年11月18日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウィングでHelene Greenwayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1609年7月13日にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウエンドバーで59歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウエンドバーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Peter Patricke Foster(이)가 1550년 영국 벅잉함실르 웬도버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guy Foster(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Church(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1573년 11월 18일 영국 벅잉함실르 윙에서 Helene Greenway(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1609년 7월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 웬도버에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 웬도버에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Peter Patricke Foster nasceu em 1550, em Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Guy Foster, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Joan Church, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Helene Greenway em 18 de novembro de 1573, em Wing, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 13 de julho de 1609, em Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Peter Patricke Foster родился в 1550 г. в местности вендовир, буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Guy Foster было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Church, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Helene Greenway 18 ноября 1573 г. в местности виг, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 13 июля 1609 г. в местности вендовир, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности вендовир, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Peter Patricke Foster nació en 1550, en Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Guy Foster, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Joan Church, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Helene Greenway el 18 de noviembre de 1573, en Wing, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 13 de julio de 1609, en Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
